swift_test.result: exit 0, all suites pass; SQLCipherMigrationTests: 11 tests passed
swift_test.note: Commit message and ADR-025 claim 12 new tests; actual count is 11.

verification.encryption_evidence: Test sqlCipherDatabaseCannotBeOpenedWithoutKey proves opening the file without a key throws at DatabaseQueue init (page-level encryption real, not a no-op); sqlCipherDatabaseOpensWithCorrectKey proves round-trip.
verification.trailers: SDL-Commit-Author: capability-apple-native-development; SDL-Routing-Attestation: 25; Task-Ref: issue-25; Lifecycle-Record-ID b7b5b906 matches routing/25.json node_id. All changed files within attestation allowed_paths.
verification.ci: sdl-governance check FAILING on this PR and on all other recent branches (issue-16, issue-23): workflow expects .stibdedlom/bin/validate-schemas.py but .stibdedlom/bin is an uninitialized git submodule in CI checkout. Pre-existing, repo-wide; not caused by this PR.

findings.issue: GRDB dependency is an absolute local path (/Users/vishalsingh/Documents/v-i-s-h-a-l/github/GRDB.swift-sqlcipher). No other machine or CI can resolve the package graph; merging to main makes the repo unbuildable for anyone but the author.
findings.remediation: Publish the fork (tag v7.10.0-sqlcipher-council-1) to a remote URL and pin it, or use a repo-relative path with a documented bootstrap step, before merge.

findings.issue: CouncilApp still pins remote groue/GRDB.swift 7.9.0 and links product GRDB from it while also depending on ../Council, which now vendors a different package also exporting module GRDB. Two modules named GRDB in one build graph breaks the CouncilApp SwiftPM build on this branch.
findings.remediation: Drop the direct GRDB dependency from CouncilApp/Package.swift (app sources do not import GRDB) or point it at the same fork. Committed xcodeproj has no GRDB references and is unaffected. CouncilApp was outside the routing attestation's allowed_paths, so this needs a follow-up attestation or an expanded one.

findings.issue: SQLCipherMigration is never invoked from production code. RuntimeAssembly opens stores with useSQLCipher defaulting to true, so an existing plaintext memory.sqlite from a previous install fails to open (SQLITE_NOTADB) at startup with no migration attempted.
findings.remediation: Wire detectLegacyDatabase/migrate into store startup (CLI and app), or explicitly document that wiring is deferred to a follow-up issue and that this release breaks pre-existing databases.

findings.issue: detectLegacyDatabase relies solely on the .sqlcipher marker file. Fresh encrypted databases created via GRDBMemoryStore.make/GRDBAuditLog.make never get a marker, so they are misdetected as legacy plaintext; a future auto-migration pass would try to open an encrypted DB without a key and fail.
findings.remediation: Write the marker in make() when useSQLCipher is true, and/or strengthen detection by checking for the 'SQLite format 3\0' file header instead of relying only on a sidecar marker.

findings.issue: The same HKDF output (info com.council.memory.database.v1) is used both as the SQLCipher raw page key and as the column-level AES-256-GCM key. No plaintext leak, but key separation between the two layers is lost.
findings.remediation: Derive a distinct subkey for the SQLCipher layer (e.g. info com.council.memory.sqlcipher.v1) so page-level and column-level keys are independent.

findings.issue: Migration swap/rollback does not handle -wal/-shm sidecar files; a stale plaintext WAL next to the target path would be replayed into the freshly swapped encrypted database. Low risk with DatabaseQueue (non-WAL default) but a real corruption vector after crashes.
findings.remediation: Checkpoint and delete sidecar files for legacyPath before the swap, and for path during rollback.

findings.issue: migrate(legacyPath:profileKey:salt:) requires the caller to pass the same salt persisted in salt.bin; any other salt yields an encrypted DB that make() cannot reopen (it resolves salt.bin). Precondition is undocumented and migrate does not resolve/persist the salt itself. Also no post-swap verification open.
findings.remediation: Resolve the salt from salt.bin inside migrate (or document the precondition), and open the migrated DB once after the swap to verify it decrypts before returning success.

findings.issue: 11 meaningful tests cover detection, key-less denial, correct-key round-trip, in-memory mode, migration data preservation (including encrypted column payloads and audit chain), rollback, and marker contents. Gaps: no wrong-key denial test, no marker-less-encrypted-DB detection test, no multi-record/audit-ordering migration test. Docs claim 12 tests.
findings.remediation: Add the missing negative tests; fix the test count in the commit message/ADR (11, not 12).

findings.issue: Migration intentionally retains a plaintext .pre-sqlcipher-backup after success. Documented and required for rollback, but plaintext at rest persists until an operator deletes it.
findings.remediation: Document operator guidance to delete the backup after validating the migrated store, or add a cleanup command/flag.

findings.issue: ADR header still references lifecycle record 6171148c-c5fd-4d38-bd99-786de23866ac while the implementation commit and routing attestation use b7b5b906-8db1-4eb8-a759-cece3da2a7b2. ADR status change Proposed to Accepted is otherwise justified by the implementation.
findings.remediation: Update the ADR lifecycle record reference.

findings.issue: sdl-governance CI fails on every branch because .stibdedlom/bin is a git submodule that CI does not initialize (missing validate-schemas.py). Governance gate is red repo-wide.
findings.remediation: Initialize submodules in the workflow checkout or vendor the validation scripts. Pre-existing; not introduced by this PR.

positive_notes: PRAGMA key application order is correct: file-based stores apply the raw key inside Configuration.prepareDatabase before GRDB runs any SQL; in-memory stores key via writeWithoutTransaction before migrations.
Raw-key mode (x'<64-hex>') preserves the HKDF hierarchy with no extra PBKDF2; key is hex-only so the SQL interpolation carries no injection risk.
Shared in-memory queue handling (hasExistingTables) correctly prevents double-keying when GRDBMemoryStore is constructed before GRDBAuditLog.
Migration copy preserves UUID primary keys and copies audit previousHash/hmac verbatim; audit chain integrity verified by tests; rollback restores a readable plaintext database.
No plaintext leaks in the migration temp path: .next is encrypted from its first page; swap is two moveItem operations with restore-on-failure.
Commit trailers are correct and complete; --no-verify usage is disclosed transparently in the commit body with the manual validation record.
